---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

[← Back to Projects](content.html#remote-sensing-page)

# Hyperspectral Vegetation Spectral Analysis

## Overview

This project explores hyperspectral reflectance signatures of vegetation and surface materials across the visible, near-infrared, and shortwave infrared spectral regions. Hyperspectral measurements allow detailed analysis of vegetation properties such as chlorophyll content, leaf structure, and moisture.

## Data

Field spectrometry measurements of vegetation and surface materials including:

-   Bigleaf maple
-   Douglas-fir
-   Garry oak
-   Soil
-   Grass
-   Moss
-   Coarse woody debris

## Methods

Spectral curves were analyzed across three main spectral regions:

| Region        | Wavelength   | Information        |
|---------------|--------------|--------------------|
| Visible       | 400–700 nm   | pigment absorption |
| Near infrared | 750–1300 nm  | leaf structure     |
| SWIR          | 1300–2500 nm | water content      |

## Example Code

\`\`\`r Refl_ind \<- function(wl, refl, target_wl){ approx(x = wl, y = refl, xout = target_wl, rule = 2)\$y }
